resources.string: A dull bell tolls in the fog, and polished armor remembers dust
A DULL BELL SOUNDS IN FOG. POLISHED ARMOR REMEMBERS DUST.

The bell does not hurry. With each breath of fog, the wall sheds another pale scale; with each patient drop of acid rain, proud alloy reddens, crumbles, and forgets the shape it defended. Gears unclench inside their casings. Towers lean without drama. Rivers dry to a crust of old poison. Build higher if you wish. Time owns the ladder, the stone, and the hand that lifts them.
THE BELL DOES NOT HURRY. ACID RAIN REDDENS ALLOY. GEARS UNCLENCH. TOWERS LEAN. BUILD HIGHER IF YOU WISH. TIME OWNS THE WALL.
